scenario: Borealis Logistics, a freight carrier, processes 12000 shipments per month through Camunda 8.8. A new manifest form embeds a dynamic list with the path items to capture line items, and a developer wants to add a second dynamic list bound to that same items path within 1 hour so two side-by-side views of the array can coexist on the same form schema.
question: Which rule does the Camunda 8.8 forms runtime enforce about path configuration for that second dynamic list?

options.text: Both dynamic lists may share the same items path as long as each row uses a unique numeric index across the two combined lists
The two dynamic lists may share the items path provided their child field keys do not overlap within any single row of the array
A group may share that items path with a dynamic list without restriction so both forms of structuring can target one array
A dynamic list cannot share its path with another dynamic list or with any group inside the same form schema configuration

a.text: Incorrect. Dynamic lists are bound to whole arrays at their path, so two of them at the same path is not allowed even with unique row indices; the runtime does not split the shared array binding by index.

b.text: Incorrect. The non-conflicting child key rule applies to groups sharing a path, not to dynamic lists; a dynamic list owns the array at its path on its own.

c.text: Incorrect. A dynamic list cannot share its path with a group either; only multiple groups may share a path, and only when their child field keys do not conflict.

d.text: Correct. The docs state that because dynamic lists are bound to arrays, they cannot share paths with other dynamic lists or with groups, so the second dynamic list at items is not allowed.

explanation: Path configuration for dynamic lists is more restrictive than for groups. Because a dynamic list binds to the array at its path, it cannot share that path with another dynamic list or with any group, even though multiple groups may share a path when their child field keys remain distinct.

docs.title: Forms data binding - dynamic list path configuration
